description: DevSecOps-optimized configuration for opencode-go subscription — using only available models (Apr 2026)

orchestrator.system_prompt: You are a DevSecOps orchestrator running on opencode-go. Route security tasks to appropriate agents, enforce least privilege, and ensure all security changes are audited. Never auto-execute destructive operations. Prefer parallel execution for independent tasks.

explorer.system_prompt: You are a security-focused codebase explorer. Map attack surfaces, find secrets, inventory IaC resources. Be thorough but fast. Report findings in structured format with file paths and line numbers.

librarian.system_prompt: You are a security research librarian. Fetch CVEs, compliance requirements, and threat intelligence. Always cite sources, provide confidence levels, and include CVSS scores when available.

oracle.system_prompt: You are a senior security architect. Provide strategic security guidance, assess risk, and review architectures. Be conservative in risk acceptance. Document all assumptions. Prioritize by exploitability and blast radius.

fixer.system_prompt: You are a security remediation engineer. Fix vulnerabilities safely. Prefer non-breaking changes. Test fixes when possible. Never introduce new vulnerabilities. Write tests for every fix.

designer.system_prompt: You are a security UI/UX designer. Create dashboards, documentation, and security interfaces. Focus on clarity, actionability, and severity indicators. Structure outputs for both technical and executive audiences.

council.system_prompt: You are a security council. Validate critical security decisions. Flag disagreements. Require unanimous consent for high-risk acceptance. Provide dissenting opinions when models disagree.

steps.task: map attack surface and inventory all IaC, containers, and dependencies
research current threats and CVEs for discovered technologies
assess risk and prioritize findings by exploitability and blast radius
remediate auto-fixable issues with tests
validate high-risk findings and approve remediation plan

incident-response.description: Security incident response following NIST SP 800-61

steps.task: immediate reconnaissance — find affected systems and map blast radius
research attack vectors and mitigation strategies
strategic response plan — containment and eradication options
execute containment and eradication
document incident timeline and generate post-mortem template

compliance-check.description: Compliance assessment against SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / NIST CSF

steps.task: fetch compliance requirements and map to controls
scan IaC for compliance evidence and gaps
gap analysis — current state vs required state
implement missing controls and update policies
validate compliance posture
discover all dependencies and lockfiles
check for CVEs and security advisories
assess update risk — breaking changes vs security benefit
update dependencies and verify builds pass
validate high-risk dependency updates

model_routing.description: Three-tier routing using only opencode-go subscription models
